title: SysBio FAIRplex Genome-Wide Association Studies
body: Explore visualizations of Genome-Wide Association Studies (GWAS) within cohorts from the AMP-AD and AMP-PDRD projects. Case versus case Genome-Wide Association Studies (GWAS) were performed on 561 individuals with Alzheimer's Disease (AD) from AMP-AD versus 2542 individuals with Parkinson's disease or related disorders (PDRD) from AMP-PDRD. The current interface is a small proof of concept that will be expanded with additional datasets in the future. Learn more about the underlying datasets or data harmonization procedures. In the Manhattan plot, the x-axis represents the genome as if all chromosomes were laid end-to-end, ordered by their chromosome number. The y-axis shows the -log10(p-value) of each association. Each point represents a variant. In the Quantile-Quantile (Q-Q) probability plot, the x-axis represents the expected quantiles of all associations under a uniform distribution function, and the y-axis shows the actual values.
